_comment: Era rule for 'A Última Vez' stage tracking. Stored as data, not code branches (brief §2). Stages are defined by TEAMS REMAINING, not match names: quartas = top 8, semi = top 4, final = top 2. The build script (scripts/build-nations.mjs) reads these maps; it does not hardcode stage years. Validated against jfjelstul/worldcup matches.csv on 2026-06-03 (all gates pass).

trackedBuckets._comment: stage_name -> tracked drought bucket (qf/sf/final). Powers lastStage in the table. 'second group stage' (1974/78/82) = quartas equivalent; 'final round' (1950) = semi credit for all four; 'third-place match' = semi credit (you were a top-4 team). round of 16 / group stage are below the tracking threshold and intentionally absent.

fineStages._comment: stage_name -> full-granularity deepest stage for the country-page timeline (runs[]) and best{}. Same era mapping as trackedBuckets, extended downward with r16 and group. 'champion' is not a stage_name; it is derived from the winner of the 'final' match (see specialCases.champions).

specialCases._comment: 1950 had no single final match: a final round-robin of 4 decided the title. All four (Brazil, Uruguay, Sweden, Spain) get semi credit via 'final round'. The decisive Uruguay 2-1 Brazil match = the final for both, and Uruguay = champion. This is the ONLY hand-coded year; every other stage comes straight from the CSV.
